title: GitLab Agile Portfolio Management - Hands-On Lab: Access The GitLab Training Environment
content:
GitLab Agile Portfolio Management - Hands-On Lab: Access The GitLab Training Environment

Estimated time to complete: 5 to 10 minutes

Estimated time to complete: 5 to 10 minutes

In this lab, you will access the training lab environment. This environment will allow you to try all of the GitLab Ultimate features, providing you with an environment to follow along with all of the lab steps.

Task A. Access your Training Group and Lab Environment

Navigate to https://gitlabdemo.com/invite in a web browser.

Navigate to https://gitlabdemo.com/invite in a web browser.

In the Invitation Code field, enter the invitation code provided by your instructor or in the LevelUp LMS.

In the Invitation Code field, enter the invitation code provided by your instructor or in the LevelUp LMS.

Select Provision Training Environment . The login details for accessing the environment are slightly different between self-paced training and instructor-led training. Please follow the instructions that suit your needs.

Select Provision Training Environment .

The login details for accessing the environment are slightly different between self-paced training and instructor-led training. Please follow the instructions that suit your needs.

The login details for accessing the environment are slightly different between self-paced training and instructor-led training. Please follow the instructions that suit your needs.

For self-paced courses, you will need your GitLab.com username. To find your username, navigate to Gitlab .

For self-paced courses, you will need your GitLab.com username. To find your username, navigate to Gitlab .

In the left sidebar, select your profile image.

In the left sidebar, select your profile image.

In the resulting dropdown, you will see a value starting with @ . This is your GitLab username.

In the resulting dropdown, you will see a value starting with @ . This is your GitLab username.

After inputting your invitation code, the portal prompts you for your GitLab.com username. Enter your GitLab.com user (excluding the leading @ sign) in the field provided.

After inputting your invitation code, the portal prompts you for your GitLab.com username. Enter your GitLab.com user (excluding the leading @ sign) in the field provided.

Select Provision Training Environment .

Select Provision Training Environment .

Select My Group at the bottom of the page.

Select My Group at the bottom of the page.

Sign in with your GitLab.com credentials.

Sign in with your GitLab.com credentials.

You will be redirected to a My Test Group group that provides a sandbox for you to perform training lab steps in. This group has a GitLab Ultimate license to see all of the features while your personal username namespace requires a paid subscription or a free trial to access all of the features. If you receive a 404 error when accessing your group, it is likely due to the username input during the lab provisioning. Double check your GitLab username to make sure it is entered correctly.

You will be redirected to a My Test Group group that provides a sandbox for you to perform training lab steps in.

This group has a GitLab Ultimate license to see all of the features while your personal username namespace requires a paid subscription or a free trial to access all of the features. If you receive a 404 error when accessing your group, it is likely due to the username input during the lab provisioning. Double check your GitLab username to make sure it is entered correctly.

This group has a GitLab Ultimate license to see all of the features while your personal username namespace requires a paid subscription or a free trial to access all of the features.

If you receive a 404 error when accessing your group, it is likely due to the username input during the lab provisioning. Double check your GitLab username to make sure it is entered correctly.

From your My Test Group training subgroup, click the New project button.

From your My Test Group training subgroup, click the New project button.

If you’d like to suggest changes to this lab, please submit them via merge request.
